_doc: Feature-centric view: every signal, context layer, and condition that contributes to each feature's score. Direct signals add their weight to this feature only. Universal signals add weight × 0.4 to ALL features equally. Mindset and prompt synthesis vary per user.

ai-models.name: Better AI Models

universalSignals._note: Each universal signal contributes its weight × 0.4 to this feature

contextLayers.mindsetVector: Varies by role × stakes. High in: Engineering|internal (2), Student|internal (2), Product|internal (2). Low in: Sales|high-external (2), Marketing|high-external (2).
contextLayers.promptSynthesis: LLM scores 0-5 based on prompt content. High for: editing-heavy, quality-focused, AI-related prompts.

ai-models.typicalScenario: User is heavily editing slides, struggling with quality, re-editing the same content repeatedly.

brand-kit.name: Brand Kit

universalSignals._note: Each universal signal contributes its weight × 0.4 to this feature

contextLayers.mindsetVector: Varies by role × stakes. High in: Design|high-external (5), Creator|high-external (4), Marketing|high-external (4). Low in: Engineering|internal (0), Finance|internal (0).
contextLayers.promptSynthesis: LLM scores 0-5. High for: brand identity, portfolio, agency credentials, design-focused prompts.

brand-kit.typicalScenario: User mentions their brand in the prompt, changes fonts/styles, switches templates, works across multiple decks.

unbranded.name: Unbranded Pro Templates

universalSignals._note: Each universal signal contributes its weight × 0.4 to this feature

contextLayers.mindsetVector: Varies by role × stakes. High in: Design|high-external (4), Sales|high-external (4), Marketing|high-external (4), Creator|high-external (4). Low in: Engineering|internal (0), Operations|internal (0).
contextLayers.promptSynthesis: LLM scores 0-5. High for: investor pitches, enterprise proposals, portfolio decks, client-facing work.

unbranded.typicalScenario: User is sharing/presenting to external stakeholders, previewing slideshow, using templates for client-facing work.

export.name: PowerPoint/PDF Export

universalSignals._note: Each universal signal contributes its weight × 0.4 to this feature

contextLayers.mindsetVector: Varies by role × stakes. High in: Data Analytics|high-external (4), Finance|high-external (4), Teacher|high-external (4), Student|high-external (4). Low in: most internal roles (1).
contextLayers.audienceStakes: No direct stakes signal for export
contextLayers.promptSynthesis: LLM scores 0-5. High for: thesis defense, investor meetings, enterprise proposals, anything needing offline delivery.

export.typicalScenario: User tries to download (hits paywall), plays slideshow, has previously bought a single export, needs to email the deck.

invite-collab.name: Invite Collaborators

universalSignals._note: Each universal signal contributes its weight × 0.4 to this feature

contextLayers.mindsetVector: Varies by role × stakes. High in: Product|internal (4), Engineering|internal (3), Operations|internal (3), Consulting|internal (3). Low in: Design|high-external (0), Creator|internal (1).
contextLayers.promptSynthesis: LLM scores 0-5. High for: team restructuring, product roadmap, group projects, collaborative work.

invite-collab.typicalScenario: User uses team/we/our language in prompt, creates large decks, works in internal/collaborative context.

analytics.name: Viewer Analytics

universalSignals._note: Each universal signal contributes its weight × 0.4 to this feature

contextLayers.mindsetVector: Varies by role × stakes. High in: Sales|high-external (4), Data Analytics|high-external (4), Leadership|high-external (4). Low in: Student|internal (0), Teacher|internal (0).
contextLayers.promptSynthesis: LLM scores 0-5. High for: enterprise proposals, ROI case studies, partnership pitches — anything where tracking engagement matters.

analytics.typicalScenario: User shares decks, adds data/charts, presents to external high-stakes audiences where follow-up tracking matters.

gen-speed.name: Generation Speed

universalSignals._note: Each universal signal contributes its weight × 0.4 to this feature

contextLayers.mindsetVector: Varies by role × stakes. High in: Leadership|internal (3), Operations|internal (3), Engineering|internal (3), Student|internal (3). Low in: high-external roles typically 1.
contextLayers.promptSynthesis: LLM scores 0-5. High for: internal reviews, OKR planning, quick internal work. NOT related to urgency.

gen-speed.typicalScenario: User generates large decks, spends 20+ min in editor, creates multiple decks — they'd benefit from faster generation, not because they're in a rush but because they use the tool heavily.

pres-refresh.name: Presentation Refresh

universalSignals._note: Each universal signal contributes its weight × 0.4 to this feature

contextLayers.mindsetVector: Varies by role × stakes. High in: Product|internal (3), Engineering|internal (2), Teacher|internal (3), Data Analytics|internal (2). Low in: most high-external roles (0).
contextLayers.audienceStakes: No direct stakes signal for pres-refresh
contextLayers.promptSynthesis: LLM scores 0-5. High for: Q1 reviews, OKR planning, product roadmaps — iterative internal work.

pres-refresh.typicalScenario: User previews then re-edits, manages multiple decks, rearranges slides — they're iterating on content and would benefit from AI-powered refresh.

hire-team.name: Hire Our Team

universalSignals._note: Each universal signal contributes its weight × 0.4 to this feature

contextLayers.mindsetVector: Varies by role × stakes. High in: Leadership|high-external (2), Sales|high-external (2), Consulting|high-external (2). Low in: most internal roles (0).
contextLayers.promptSynthesis: LLM scores 0-5. High for: urgent deadlines, struggle language ('help me', 'can't'), high-stakes presentations.

hire-team.typicalScenario: User is struggling — re-editing repeatedly, undo/redo, switching templates, high-stakes audience. The system detects frustration and offers the service team instead of a feature unlock.

distinctBehavior.nudgeFraming: Let us handle it (not 'unlock this feature')
distinctBehavior.routesTo: Service booking flow (not Pro pricing page)
